what_is_open: For beta<gamma, determine the sharp central-(epsilon_n,delta_n)-DP minimax absolute risk over the full locally positive-density observational Holder class. This includes determining whether its privacy-free limit is r_n^CATE on that full class and whether the formal (alpha+beta)-privacy power, or a different nuisance/private-Gram power, is an attainable and necessary regime.
obstruction: The certified full-class lower and upper bounds have different smoothness exponents. Complete central-DP achievability at the formal higher-order objective would require privacy or global stability for nuisance pilots, the local Gram estimate and inverse, first-order scores, and the second-order fold, together with control of the KBRW random-design Gram/density third-order remainder under only the frozen local density floor. Those controls are not consequences of the frozen assumptions. For the converse, thm:causal-dp-two-point-barrier rules out obtaining the larger q=alpha+beta<gamma privacy separation through the ordinary two-point certificate n{exp(epsilon_n)-1+delta_n}TV(P,Q)<=eta. A same-class converse therefore needs a comparison beyond this route, for example a positive-density multi-hypothesis or fuzzy construction with a proved approximate-DP tracing inequality. Neither achievability nor necessity of the q branch is presently proved.
attempted: On the upper side, foldwise accounting shows that privatizing only the terminal score does not protect records entering nuisance or Gram computations; separately privatizing those objects creates error terms omitted from E_n^alg, while treating them as nonprivate internal computations violates central DP. The explicit armwise private local-polynomial estimator gives the uniform beta-smooth upper endpoint. On the lower side, the localized causal two-point family gives the nonprivate gamma branch and the gamma privacy branch. Extending the interpolation argument to arbitrary design densities and propensities yields the theorem-level two-point barrier. A positive-floor paired propensity/outcome microcell program still lacks a mixture-divergence cancellation and a matching approximate-DP hypercube inequality, so asserting its q-scale behavior would assume the crux.
partial_result: The primary established result is thm:causal-dp-two-point-barrier: for arbitrary P,Q in the frozen causal class, TV(P,Q)>=c|tau_P(x_0)-tau_Q(x_0)|^{1+d/gamma}, so no two-point argument using the displayed DP TV-contraction certificate can establish more than the gamma privacy separation (n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}. This is a proof-method hardness theorem, not a claim against fuzzy or multi-hypothesis methods. As secondary calibration, lem:certified-private-cate-bracket proves c{n^{-gamma/(2gamma+d)} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-gamma/(gamma+d)}} <= R_n^DP <= C{n^{-beta/(2beta+d)} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-beta/(beta+d)}}. The bracket is sharp when beta=gamma and when 1<=n epsilon_n<=C_0. Lem:equal-smoothness-regression-inheritance records that the beta=gamma exponent is inherited from one-server private pointwise regression: the upper bound differences two armwise private regressions and the causal lower family embeds one regression subproblem, with overlap affecting only constants. The formal optimized value n^{-kappa} vee (n epsilon_n)^{-min(gamma,alpha+beta)/(min(gamma,alpha+beta)+d)} remains algebraic only. The full beta<gamma frontier, including achievability and converse for any nuisance-privacy branch and the privacy-free full-positive-density limit, remains open.
